title: frasera bimaculata
intro: Frasera bimaculata, also known as two-spotted sage or Indian sage, is a perennial herbaceous plant native to North America. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is used to treat a variety of ailments, including sore throats, coughs, and skin irritations. The plant's roots, leaves, and stems contain iridoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, providing potential health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and promoting wound healing. Frasera bimaculata can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in herbal medicine to support respiratory health and soothe skin conditions.
